title.es: Seguro Medico para Contratistas 1099 en 2026 | CoveredUSA

description.en: ACA plans, Form 7206 deduction, HSA strategy, and SEP triggers for 1099 independent contractors in 2026. Subsidy cliff is back at 400% FPL.
description.es: Planes ACA, deduccion Formulario 7206, HSA y eventos SEP para contratistas independientes 1099 en 2026.

h1.en: Health Insurance for 1099 Independent Contractors in 2026
h1.es: Seguro Medico para Contratistas Independientes 1099 en 2026

subhero.en: As a 1099 contractor, freelancer, or sole proprietor, you pay every dollar of your premium out of pocket, but the IRS gives you tools W-2 employees don't get: a 100% above-the-line deduction on Form 7206 and an HSA triple tax advantage that can cut your effective cost by 30% to 50%.
subhero.es: Como contratista independiente, freelancer o autonomo, paga cada centavo de su prima de su bolsillo, pero el IRS le da herramientas que los empleados W-2 no tienen: una deduccion del 100% above-the-line en el Formulario 7206 y la ventaja triple del HSA que puede reducir su costo efectivo entre 30% y 50%.

quickAnswer.en: 1099 contractors have four main coverage options in 2026: an ACA marketplace plan with premium tax credits (if MAGI is under 400% FPL at $63,840 single), an HSA-qualified HDHP above the subsidy cliff, a spouse's employer plan, or COBRA from a prior job. Form 7206 lets sole proprietors deduct 100% of premiums above the line, cutting income tax only, not self-employment tax on Schedule SE.
quickAnswer.es: Los contratistas 1099 tienen cuatro opciones principales de cobertura en 2026: un plan del Mercado ACA con creditos fiscales (si el MAGI esta bajo 400% FPL en $63,840 individual), un HDHP compatible con HSA por encima del tope del subsidio, el plan del empleador del conyuge, o COBRA de un trabajo anterior. El Formulario 7206 permite a los propietarios unicos deducir el 100% de las primas above-the-line, reduciendo solo el impuesto sobre la renta, no el impuesto al trabajo independiente en el Schedule SE.

introParagraphs.en: 1099 independent contractors, freelancers, and sole proprietors share a common reality: no employer picks up half the premium. Every dollar of health insurance cost lands on you. But the federal tax code balances that with tools unavailable to W-2 workers: the Form 7206 above-the-line deduction lets a self-employed contract worker write off 100% of premiums before calculating taxable income, and pairing a High-Deductible Health Plan with a Health Savings Account stacks another deduction on top. A Schedule C filer earning $75,000 in gross 1099 income can realistically see $55,000 to $60,000 in MAGI after business expenses, the half-SE-tax deduction, and the Form 7206 deduction, which puts a sizable premium tax credit within reach even at a comfortable income.
introParagraphs.es: Los contratistas independientes 1099, freelancers y propietarios unicos comparten una realidad comun: ningun empleador paga la mitad de la prima. Cada dolar del costo del seguro medico recae sobre usted. Pero el codigo fiscal federal lo compensa con herramientas no disponibles para los empleados W-2: la deduccion above-the-line del Formulario 7206 permite a un trabajador por contrato independiente deducir el 100% de las primas antes de calcular el ingreso imponible, y combinar un Plan con Deducible Alto con una Cuenta de Ahorros para la Salud apila otra deduccion adicional. Un declarante de Schedule C que gana $75,000 en ingresos brutos 1099 puede ver de manera realista un MAGI de $55,000 a $60,000 despues de los gastos comerciales, la deduccion de la mitad del impuesto SE y la deduccion del Formulario 7206, lo que pone un credito fiscal de prima significativo al alcance incluso con un ingreso comodo.

introParagraphs.en: Every independent contractor, freelance consultant, Schedule C filer, or sole proprietor reading this page faces the same structural reality: no W-2 employer to split the premium, no payroll deduction pretax savings, and variable income that makes subsidy projection harder than it looks. Coverage options run the gamut from subsidized ACA marketplace plans for lower-earning contract workers to HSA-paired HDHPs for higher-earning freelancers above the subsidy cliff. Rideshare and app-based delivery workers who also receive platform-specific stipends under California Proposition 22 will find deeper mechanics on the rideshare drivers guide.
introParagraphs.es: Todo contratista independiente, consultor freelancer, declarante de Schedule C o propietario unico que lee esta pagina enfrenta la misma realidad estructural: no hay empleador W-2 que divida la prima, no hay ahorro de nomina antes de impuestos, y el ingreso variable hace que la proyeccion del subsidio sea mas dificil de lo que parece. Las opciones de cobertura van desde planes del Mercado ACA subsidiados para los trabajadores por contrato de menores ingresos hasta HDHP con HSA para freelancers de mayores ingresos por encima del tope del subsidio. Los conductores de rideshare y trabajadores de entrega que tambien reciben estipendios especificos de la plataforma bajo la Proposicion 22 de California encontraran mecanicas mas detalladas en la guia de conductores de rideshare.

en: ACA Marketplace with premium tax credits
Contractors with MAGI under 400% FPL ($63,840 single)
es: Mercado ACA con creditos fiscales de prima
Contratistas con MAGI bajo 400% FPL ($63,840 individual)

en: Self-employed earners above the 400% FPL subsidy cliff
es: HDHP compatible con HSA a precio completo
Autonomos por encima del tope del subsidio 400% FPL

en: Recently left employer to go independent (max 18 months)
es: Recien dejo empleador para ser independiente (max 18 meses)

footnote.en: The ACA enhanced subsidies from ARPA/IRA expired January 1, 2026. The 400% FPL subsidy cliff is back. Subsidies phase down as income climbs and stop entirely at 400% FPL. Above that line, independent contractors pay full sticker price. Form 7206 deductions can shift your effective MAGI meaningfully below gross 1099 income.
footnote.es: Los subsidios mejorados del ACA de ARPA/IRA expiraron el 1 de enero de 2026. El tope del subsidio del 400% FPL ha regresado. Los subsidios disminuyen a medida que el ingreso sube y se detienen completamente al 400% FPL. Por encima de ese limite, los contratistas independientes pagan el precio completo. Las deducciones del Formulario 7206 pueden reducir su MAGI efectivo significativamente por debajo del ingreso bruto 1099.

heading.en: Option 1: ACA Marketplace Plan with Premium Tax Credits
heading.es: Opcion 1: Plan del Mercado ACA con Creditos Fiscales de Prima

paragraphs.en: ACA Marketplace coverage is the default path for most 1099 independent contractors and freelancers without access to an employer plan. If your projected 2026 MAGI falls below 400% of the Federal Poverty Level, you qualify for the Premium Tax Credit (PTC). For a single filer that threshold is $63,840; for a household of four it is $132,000. The subsidy cliff returned January 1, 2026 when the enhanced ARPA/IRA credits expired, so subsidies phase down approaching 400% FPL and stop entirely at that line. Earning one dollar over the cliff costs you the entire credit for the year at tax-time reconciliation.
paragraphs.es: La cobertura del Mercado ACA es el camino predeterminado para la mayoria de los contratistas independientes 1099 y freelancers sin acceso a un plan del empleador. Si su MAGI proyectado para 2026 cae por debajo del 400% del Nivel de Pobreza Federal, califica para el Credito Fiscal de Prima (PTC). Para un declarante individual ese umbral es $63,840; para un hogar de cuatro personas es $132,000. El tope del subsidio regreso el 1 de enero de 2026 cuando expiraron los creditos mejorados de ARPA/IRA, por lo que los subsidios disminuyen al acercarse al 400% FPL y se detienen completamente en ese nivel.

paragraphs.en: Variable income makes MAGI projection harder for self-employed contract workers than for W-2 employees. The marketplace requires a projection at enrollment; the IRS reconciles the advance credits against actual MAGI on Form 1095-A at tax time. Underestimate your income and you owe money back; overestimate and the IRS refunds the difference. For sole proprietors, the MAGI-lowering stack works in your favor: gross 1099 income minus business expenses, minus half of self-employment tax, minus Form 7206 health insurance premiums, minus HSA contributions on Schedule 1. A contract worker with $85,000 in gross 1099 billings can land at $60,000 to $65,000 in MAGI with those deductions, which puts Silver-plan cost-sharing reductions in range at 250% FPL or below.
paragraphs.es: El ingreso variable hace que la proyeccion del MAGI sea mas dificil para los trabajadores por contrato independientes que para los empleados W-2. El mercado requiere una proyeccion al momento de la inscripcion; el IRS concilia los creditos anticipados contra el MAGI real en el Formulario 1095-A al momento de declarar impuestos. Subestime su ingreso y debera devolver dinero; sobreestime y el IRS le reembolsara la diferencia. Para los propietarios unicos, la pila de reduccion del MAGI trabaja a su favor: ingreso bruto 1099 menos gastos comerciales, menos la mitad del impuesto al trabajo independiente, menos las primas de seguro medico del Formulario 7206, menos las contribuciones al HSA en el Schedule 1.

heading.en: Option 2: HSA-Qualified HDHP at Full Price
heading.es: Opcion 2: HDHP Compatible con HSA a Precio Completo

paragraphs.en: For independent contractors and self-employed earners above the 400% FPL subsidy cliff, an HSA-qualified High-Deductible Health Plan is frequently the most cost-effective choice. The minimum deductible for an HSA-qualifying HDHP in 2026 is $1,700 for self-only coverage and $3,400 for family coverage (per IRS Revenue Procedure 2025-19). The HDHP maximum out-of-pocket in 2026 is $8,500 self-only / $17,000 family. HDHPs typically carry the lowest sticker premiums on the ACA Marketplace, and pairing one with a Health Savings Account adds a triple tax advantage unavailable through any other vehicle.
paragraphs.es: Para los contratistas independientes y autonomos por encima del tope del subsidio del 400% FPL, un Plan con Deducible Alto compatible con HSA es frecuentemente la opcion mas rentable. El deducible minimo para un HDHP compatible con HSA en 2026 es $1,700 para cobertura individual y $3,400 para cobertura familiar (segun el Procedimiento de Ingresos IRS 2025-19). El maximo de bolsillo del HDHP en 2026 es $8,500 individual / $17,000 familiar. Los HDHP tipicamente tienen las primas de etiqueta mas bajas en el Mercado ACA.

paragraphs.en: A 1099 contractor or freelancer who opens an HSA alongside an HDHP can contribute up to $4,400 for self-only coverage or $8,750 for family coverage in 2026, plus a $1,000 catch-up contribution if age 55 or older. Those contributions are deductible above the line on Schedule 1 (Form 8889), reducing MAGI for the following year's ACA subsidy calculation. Growth inside the HSA is tax-free. Qualified medical withdrawals are tax-free. A sole proprietor in the 24% federal bracket who maxes the self-only HSA saves roughly $1,056 in federal income tax on the contribution alone. Flexible Spending Accounts (FSAs) are employer-sponsored and not available to 1099 contractors or self-employed workers who have no W-2 employer.
paragraphs.es: Un contratista 1099 o freelancer que abre una HSA junto con un HDHP puede contribuir hasta $4,400 para cobertura individual o $8,750 para cobertura familiar en 2026, mas una contribucion adicional de $1,000 si tiene 55 anos o mas. Esas contribuciones son deducibles above-the-line en el Schedule 1 (Formulario 8889), reduciendo el MAGI para el calculo del subsidio ACA del ano siguiente. El crecimiento dentro de la HSA es libre de impuestos. Los retiros medicos calificados son libres de impuestos. Las Cuentas de Gastos Flexibles (FSA) son patrocinadas por empleadores y no estan disponibles para los contratistas 1099 o trabajadores independientes sin empleador W-2.

heading.es: Opcion 3: Plan del Empleador del Conyuge

paragraphs.en: Joining a spouse's W-2 employer plan is frequently the lowest total-cost option for married independent contractors and freelancers. Employer coverage is paid pretax through payroll withholding, which also reduces the employed spouse's FICA liability in ways the Form 7206 deduction does not. The catch: enrollment is only available during the employer's open enrollment window, or within 60 days of a qualifying life event such as marriage, birth of a child, or losing other coverage. If the spouse's employer charges a high dependent premium (above roughly $400 to $500 per month), an ACA Marketplace plan with a premium tax credit may still beat it on net cost.
paragraphs.es: Unirse al plan del empleador W-2 del conyuge es frecuentemente la opcion de menor costo total para los contratistas independientes y freelancers casados. La cobertura del empleador se paga antes de impuestos a traves de la retencion de nomina. La advertencia: la inscripcion solo esta disponible durante la ventana de inscripcion abierta del empleador, o dentro de los 60 dias de un evento calificador como matrimonio, nacimiento de un hijo o perdida de otra cobertura. Si el empleador del conyuge cobra una prima alta por dependientes (por encima de aproximadamente $400 a $500 por mes), un plan del Mercado ACA con un credito fiscal de prima aun puede ganar en costo neto.

heading.en: Option 4: COBRA from a Prior W-2 Job
heading.es: Opcion 4: COBRA de un Trabajo W-2 Anterior

paragraphs.en: Contractors who recently left W-2 employment can continue their old employer's group plan through COBRA for up to 18 months. The cost is steep: you now pay the full employer plus employee premium share plus a 2% administrative fee. A plan that cost $180 per month as an employee contribution can easily cost $1,200 to $1,500 per month on COBRA. The one practical use case: if you are mid-treatment with a specialist or mid-authorization for a procedure and switching plans would reset deductibles or lose the provider, COBRA preserves continuity. Most self-employed contract workers find an ACA Marketplace plan with a premium tax credit cheaper within the first month, since leaving W-2 employment is itself a qualifying life event that triggers a 60-day Marketplace Special Enrollment Period.
paragraphs.es: Los contratistas que recientemente dejaron el empleo W-2 pueden continuar el plan grupal de su antiguo empleador a traves de COBRA hasta 18 meses. El costo es elevado: ahora paga la parte completa de la prima del empleador mas la del empleado mas una tarifa administrativa del 2%. Un plan que costaba $180 por mes como contribucion del empleado puede costar facilmente $1,200 a $1,500 por mes en COBRA. El unico caso de uso practico: si esta a mitad de un tratamiento con un especialista o a mitad de una autorizacion para un procedimiento y cambiar de plan restableceria los deducibles o perderia al proveedor. La mayoria de los trabajadores por contrato independientes encuentran un plan del Mercado ACA con un credito fiscal de prima mas barato dentro del primer mes.

intro.en: Independent contractors and self-employed workers are a heavily marketed segment. These are the products that look attractive at first glance and cause real financial harm:
intro.es: Los contratistas independientes y trabajadores autonomos son un segmento muy comercializado. Estos son los productos que parecen atractivos a primera vista y causan dano financiero real:

en: Don't have to cover pre-existing conditions, can rescind coverage retroactively, and don't count as minimum essential coverage. A single hospitalization can leave a 1099 contractor with a six-figure bill and no legal recourse.
es: Planes a corto plazo de duracion limitada

No tienen que cubrir condiciones preexistentes, pueden cancelar la cobertura retroactivamente y no cuentan como cobertura esencial minima. Una sola hospitalizacion puede dejar a un contratista 1099 con una factura de seis cifras sin recurso legal.

en: Health share ministries (Medi-Share, Liberty HealthShare, Samaritan)

NOT insurance. No legal obligation to pay claims. Pre-existing conditions excluded. Lifestyle clauses (tobacco, mental health, IVF, certain medications) can disqualify entire categories of care for freelancers who rely on them.
es: Ministerios de compartir salud (Medi-Share, Liberty, Samaritan)

NO son seguro. Sin obligacion legal de pagar reclamos. Condiciones preexistentes excluidas. Clausulas de estilo de vida pueden descalificar categorias enteras de atencion para freelancers que dependen de ellas.

en: Assuming COBRA is free because you had good employer coverage before

COBRA premiums run $600 to $1,800 per month at full group price plus 2% admin fee. Most self-employed contract workers find ACA Marketplace plans cheaper within the first month after leaving W-2 employment.
es: Asumir que COBRA es gratuito porque tenia buena cobertura del empleador antes

Las primas de COBRA oscilan entre $600 y $1,800 por mes al precio grupal completo mas un cargo administrativo del 2%. La mayoria de los trabajadores por contrato independientes encuentran planes del Mercado ACA mas baratos dentro del primer mes.

en: In 2026, subsidies phase down approaching 400% FPL and stop entirely at $63,840 for a single filer. A $1 overage costs the entire premium tax credit for the year. Use Form 7206 deductions, HSA contributions, and Solo 401(k) deferrals to manage MAGI deliberately.
es: Malinterpretar el tope del subsidio al 400% FPL

En 2026, los subsidios disminuyen al acercarse al 400% FPL y se detienen completamente en $63,840 para un declarante individual. Un exceso de $1 cuesta el credito fiscal de prima completo del ano. Use las deducciones del Formulario 7206, las contribuciones al HSA y los diferimientos del Solo 401(k) para gestionar el MAGI deliberadamente.

en: Claiming the Form 7206 deduction reduces self-employment tax

Form 7206 reduces income tax only. It does NOT reduce self-employment tax (Schedule SE, 15.3%). The 15.3% SE tax is calculated on net SE earnings before the health insurance deduction. This is the most common tax-filing mistake among first-year independent contractors.
es: Reclamar que la deduccion del Formulario 7206 reduce el impuesto al trabajo independiente

El Formulario 7206 reduce solo el impuesto sobre la renta. NO reduce el impuesto al trabajo independiente (Schedule SE, 15.3%). El 15.3% del impuesto SE se calcula sobre las ganancias netas SE antes de la deduccion del seguro medico. Es el error de declaracion de impuestos mas comun entre los contratistas independientes de primer ano.

footnote.en: Verify any plan is sold on healthcare.gov or your state exchange and covers all 10 ACA essential health benefits. Plans sold off-exchange with much lower premiums usually offer thinner coverage.
footnote.es: Verifique que cualquier plan se venda en healthcare.gov o su intercambio estatal y cubra los 10 beneficios esenciales del ACA. Los planes vendidos fuera del intercambio con primas mucho mas bajas generalmente ofrecen cobertura mas limitada.

heading.en: Premium Tax Credit (PTC) Eligibility for 1099 Contractors in 2026
heading.es: Elegibilidad del Credito Fiscal de Prima (PTC) para Contratistas 1099 en 2026

paragraphs.en: Independent contractors and self-employed workers projecting their 2026 Modified Adjusted Gross Income need one key number: 400% of the Federal Poverty Level. In 2026, that is $63,840 for a single filer and $132,000 for a household of four (based on HHS 2026 Poverty Guidelines). Below that line, the Premium Tax Credit (PTC) phases down as income climbs. Subsidies do not snap off at 250% or 300% FPL; they shrink gradually. At 400% FPL, they stop entirely. Contractors projecting income in the 350% to 400% FPL band face steeply climbing premiums with each additional dollar earned. The enhanced credits from the American Rescue Plan Act and Inflation Reduction Act expired January 1, 2026, so the subsidy cliff that was temporarily eliminated from 2021 through 2025 is fully restored.
paragraphs.es: Los contratistas independientes y trabajadores autonomos que proyectan su Ingreso Bruto Ajustado Modificado de 2026 necesitan un numero clave: el 400% del Nivel de Pobreza Federal. En 2026, eso es $63,840 para un declarante individual y $132,000 para un hogar de cuatro personas (basado en las Pautas de Pobreza HHS 2026). Por debajo de ese limite, el Credito Fiscal de Prima (PTC) disminuye a medida que el ingreso sube. Los subsidios no se cortan de golpe al 250% o 300% FPL; se reducen gradualmente. Al 400% FPL, se detienen por completo. Los contratistas que proyectan ingresos en la banda del 350% al 400% FPL enfrentan primas que suben abruptamente con cada dolar adicional ganado.

paragraphs.en: For freelancers and 1099 contract workers with variable income, MAGI projection is the central planning task. The ACA Marketplace awards advance premium tax credits monthly based on your projected MAGI. The IRS reconciles the actual credit on Form 1095-A at tax time (Schedule 2, Form 8962). Sole proprietors with self-employment income can reduce MAGI by stacking deductions: (1) deductible business expenses on Schedule C, (2) the deductible half of self-employment tax on Schedule 1, (3) the Form 7206 self-employed health insurance premium deduction, and (4) HSA contributions reported on Form 8889. A contract worker with $90,000 in gross 1099 billings can realistically project $60,000 to $70,000 in MAGI with those four deductions, which still qualifies for meaningful subsidies on a Silver plan.
paragraphs.es: Para los freelancers y trabajadores por contrato 1099 con ingresos variables, la proyeccion del MAGI es la tarea central de planificacion. El Mercado ACA otorga creditos fiscales de prima anticipados mensualmente basados en su MAGI proyectado. El IRS concilia el credito real en el Formulario 1095-A al momento de declarar impuestos (Schedule 2, Formulario 8962). Los propietarios unicos con ingresos del trabajo independiente pueden reducir el MAGI apilando deducciones: (1) gastos comerciales deducibles en el Schedule C, (2) la mitad deducible del impuesto al trabajo independiente en el Schedule 1, (3) la deduccion de primas de seguro medico del Formulario 7206, y (4) las contribuciones al HSA reportadas en el Formulario 8889.

list.en: 138% FPL ($22,025 single in 2026): Medicaid eligibility threshold in the 41 states plus D.C. that expanded Medicaid under the ACA. Below this, most 1099 contractors qualify for free Medicaid rather than Marketplace subsidies.
list.es: 138% FPL ($22,025 individual en 2026): Umbral de elegibilidad de Medicaid en los 41 estados mas D.C. que expandieron Medicaid bajo el ACA. Por debajo de esto, la mayoria de los contratistas 1099 califican para Medicaid gratuito en lugar de subsidios del Mercado.

list.en: 250% FPL ($39,900 single in 2026): Cost-sharing reduction (CSR) cutoff on Silver plans. Silver + CSR dramatically reduces deductibles and copays for income-qualifying contract workers.
list.es: 250% FPL ($39,900 individual en 2026): Limite de reduccion de costos compartidos (CSR) en planes Silver. Silver + CSR reduce dramaticamente los deducibles y copagos para los trabajadores por contrato que califican por ingresos.

list.en: 400% FPL ($63,840 single / $132,000 family of four in 2026): The subsidy cliff. Above this, 1099 contractors and freelancers pay full sticker price for any Marketplace plan.
list.es: 400% FPL ($63,840 individual / $132,000 familia de cuatro en 2026): El tope del subsidio. Por encima de esto, los contratistas 1099 y freelancers pagan el precio completo por cualquier plan del Mercado.

caption.en: 2026 ACA Subsidy Eligibility by Household Size, 1099 Contractors
caption.es: Elegibilidad de Subsidio ACA 2026 por Tamano del Hogar, Contratistas 1099

footnote.en: Based on 2026 HHS Federal Poverty Guidelines (48 contiguous states + D.C.). 400% FPL figures derived at 400x the 2026 FPL base of $15,960 for household of 1, with $5,680 increment per additional person. Alaska and Hawaii have higher FPL thresholds.
footnote.es: Basado en las Pautas de Pobreza Federal HHS 2026 (48 estados contiguos + D.C.). Las cifras del 400% FPL se derivan al 400x la base FPL 2026 de $15,960 para hogar de 1, con incremento de $5,680 por persona adicional. Alaska y Hawaii tienen umbrales FPL mas altos.

heading.en: Self-Employment Health Insurance Deduction (Form 7206) for 1099 Contractors
heading.es: Deduccion del Seguro Medico para Autonomos (Formulario 7206) para Contratistas 1099

paragraphs.en: Form 7206 is the IRS worksheet that lets independent contractors and sole proprietors deduct 100% of health insurance premiums paid for themselves, their spouse, and their dependents as an above-the-line adjustment on Schedule 1, Line 17. This includes medical, dental, and qualifying long-term care premiums. The deduction reduces adjusted gross income and, critically, reduces MAGI for the following year's ACA Premium Tax Credit calculation. A self-employed contract worker paying $900 per month in premiums ($10,800 annually) in the 22% federal bracket saves roughly $2,376 in income tax through Form 7206, and the lower MAGI may also increase next year's advance credits by an additional $1,000 to $3,000 depending on where the filer sits relative to the FPL bands.
paragraphs.es: El Formulario 7206 es la hoja de trabajo del IRS que permite a los contratistas independientes y propietarios unicos deducir el 100% de las primas de seguro medico pagadas por ellos mismos, su conyuge y sus dependientes como ajuste above-the-line en el Schedule 1, Linea 17. Esto incluye primas medicas, dentales y de cuidado a largo plazo calificadas. La deduccion reduce el ingreso bruto ajustado y, de manera critica, reduce el MAGI para el calculo del Credito Fiscal de Prima ACA del ano siguiente.

paragraphs.en: Two limits matter for 1099 contractors claiming Form 7206: (1) the deduction cannot exceed your net self-employment income minus the deductible half of self-employment tax, and (2) any month in which you or your spouse were eligible for an employer-sponsored group plan disqualifies that month's premiums from the deduction. Above all else: Form 7206 reduces income tax only. It does NOT reduce self-employment tax on Schedule SE. The 15.3% self-employment tax (12.4% Social Security plus 2.9% Medicare) is calculated on net Schedule C earnings before the health insurance deduction is applied. Claiming it reduces SE tax is the single most common Form 7206 misunderstanding among new independent contractors and freelancers. For 1099 workers above the subsidy cliff, combining Form 7206 with a maxed HSA and a Solo 401(k) deferral can stack three above-the-line deductions that meaningfully reduce effective income tax and push MAGI back below the 400% FPL cliff.
paragraphs.es: Dos limites importan para los contratistas 1099 que reclaman el Formulario 7206: (1) la deduccion no puede exceder su ingreso neto del trabajo independiente menos la mitad deducible del impuesto al trabajo independiente, y (2) cualquier mes en que usted o su conyuge fueran elegibles para un plan grupal patrocinado por un empleador descalifica las primas de ese mes de la deduccion. Sobre todo: el Formulario 7206 reduce solo el impuesto sobre la renta. NO reduce el impuesto al trabajo independiente en el Schedule SE. El 15.3% del impuesto SE (12.4% del Seguro Social mas 2.9% de Medicare) se calcula sobre las ganancias netas del Schedule C antes de que se aplique la deduccion del seguro medico. Reclamar que reduce el impuesto SE es el malentendido mas comun del Formulario 7206 entre los nuevos contratistas independientes y freelancers.

heading.en: HSA and HDHP Fit for 1099 Contractors in 2026
heading.es: Compatibilidad de HSA y HDHP para Contratistas 1099 en 2026

paragraphs.en: Health Savings Accounts are available to any 1099 contractor, freelancer, or self-employed worker enrolled in a qualifying High-Deductible Health Plan. The HSA is not employer-sponsored and is fully portable, surviving job changes, contract transitions, and income swings without penalty. For 2026, the HDHP minimum deductible is $1,700 for self-only coverage and $3,400 for family coverage (IRS Revenue Procedure 2025-19). The HDHP maximum out-of-pocket is $8,500 self-only / $17,000 family. The HSA contribution limit for 2026 is $4,400 for self-only coverage and $8,750 for family coverage, plus a $1,000 catch-up for account holders age 55 or older. HSA contributions are deductible above the line on Schedule 1 via Form 8889, regardless of whether the contractor itemizes deductions.
paragraphs.es: Las Cuentas de Ahorros para la Salud (HSA) estan disponibles para cualquier contratista 1099, freelancer o trabajador autonomo inscrito en un Plan con Deducible Alto calificado. La HSA no es patrocinada por un empleador y es totalmente portatil, sobreviviendo cambios de trabajo, transiciones de contratos y fluctuaciones de ingresos sin penalidad. Para 2026, el deducible minimo del HDHP es $1,700 para cobertura individual y $3,400 para cobertura familiar (Procedimiento de Ingresos IRS 2025-19). El maximo de bolsillo del HDHP es $8,500 individual / $17,000 familiar. El limite de contribucion a la HSA para 2026 es $4,400 para cobertura individual y $8,750 para cobertura familiar, mas $1,000 adicionales para titulares de cuentas de 55 anos o mas.

paragraphs.en: The HSA triple tax advantage works particularly well for self-employed contract workers and freelancers: contributions reduce taxable income above the line (lowering both income tax and MAGI for next year's ACA subsidies), the account balance grows tax-free, and qualified medical withdrawals are tax-free. After age 65, HSA funds can be withdrawn for any purpose and taxed like a traditional IRA, effectively making the HSA a second retirement account. The Flexible Spending Account (FSA) is the alternative often confused with the HSA: FSAs are employer-only, use-it-or-lose-it accounts not available to 1099 contractors or any self-employed worker without a W-2 employer. Independent contractors should not conflate the two when researching coverage options.
paragraphs.es: La ventaja triple de impuestos de la HSA funciona particularmente bien para los trabajadores por contrato independientes y freelancers: las contribuciones reducen el ingreso imponible above-the-line (reduciendo tanto el impuesto sobre la renta como el MAGI para los subsidios ACA del proximo ano), el saldo de la cuenta crece libre de impuestos y los retiros medicos calificados son libres de impuestos. Despues de los 65 anos, los fondos de la HSA pueden retirarse para cualquier proposito y tributarse como un IRA tradicional. La Cuenta de Gastos Flexibles (FSA) es la alternativa frecuentemente confundida con la HSA: las FSA son cuentas de solo empleador, de uso o perdida, no disponibles para los contratistas 1099 o cualquier trabajador autonomo sin empleador W-2.

caption.en: 2026 HSA and HDHP Limits for 1099 Contractors
caption.es: Limites de HSA y HDHP 2026 para Contratistas 1099

es: Limite de contribucion anual a la HSA

footnote.en: Source: IRS Revenue Procedure 2025-19, May 2025. The ACA Marketplace out-of-pocket maximum in 2026 ($10,600 individual / $21,200 family) differs from the HDHP cap. Not every HDHP on the Marketplace qualifies for HSA pairing. Check that the plan label says 'HSA-eligible' or 'HSA-qualified' before opening an account.
footnote.es: Fuente: Procedimiento de Ingresos IRS 2025-19, mayo 2025. El maximo de bolsillo del Mercado ACA en 2026 ($10,600 individual / $21,200 familiar) difiere del tope del HDHP. No todos los HDHP del Mercado califican para una HSA. Verifique que la etiqueta del plan diga 'compatible con HSA' o 'calificado para HSA' antes de abrir una cuenta.

heading.en: State-Specific Programs for Independent Contractors and Freelancers
heading.es: Programas Estatales para Contratistas Independientes y Freelancers

paragraphs.en: Several states have enacted laws directly affecting health coverage access for independent contractors, freelancers, and gig workers. California's Proposition 22 (passed November 2020, effective January 2021) requires app-based gig economy platforms including Uber, Lyft, DoorDash, and Instacart to provide a quarterly healthcare stipend to drivers and couriers who average 15 or more engaged hours per week. The stipend amount is tied to the average California bronze plan premium and is prorated by engaged hours. Contractors in California who work on these platforms should claim the stipend through the platform app and may use it to offset marketplace premium costs.
paragraphs.es: Varios estados han promulgado leyes que afectan directamente el acceso a la cobertura de salud para los contratistas independientes, freelancers y trabajadores gig. La Proposicion 22 de California (aprobada en noviembre de 2020, vigente desde enero de 2021) requiere que las plataformas de economia gig basadas en aplicaciones, incluidas Uber, Lyft, DoorDash e Instacart, proporcionen un estipendio trimestral de atencion medica a los conductores y mensajeros que promediaron 15 o mas horas de trabajo comprometido por semana.

paragraphs.en: New York's Freelance Isn't Free Act (effective NYC 2017, expanded statewide August 28, 2024) requires written contracts for freelance work over $800 and creates enforcement pathways through the New York State Attorney General. While the Act does not directly provide health coverage, it ensures contract workers are paid reliably, supporting their ability to purchase Marketplace plans. Massachusetts enacted ballot Initiative 1 of 2024 (Question 3) extending portable-benefit protections to app-based workers. 1099 contractors and sole proprietors in states with individual health insurance mandates, including California, Massachusetts, New Jersey, Rhode Island, Vermont, and Washington D.C., must maintain minimum essential coverage or face a state-level tax penalty. The federal individual mandate penalty has been zero since January 1, 2019.
paragraphs.es: La Ley Freelance No es Gratis de Nueva York (efectiva en NYC desde 2017, ampliada a nivel estatal el 28 de agosto de 2024) requiere contratos escritos para trabajo freelance de mas de $800 y crea vias de cumplimiento a traves del Fiscal General del Estado de Nueva York. Massachusetts promulgo la Iniciativa 1 de las Elecciones de 2024 extendiendo protecciones de beneficios portatiles a los trabajadores basados en aplicaciones. Los contratistas 1099 y propietarios unicos en estados con mandatos individuales de seguro medico, incluidos California, Massachusetts, Nueva Jersey, Rhode Island, Vermont y Washington D.C., deben mantener una cobertura esencial minima o enfrentar una penalidad fiscal estatal. La penalidad del mandato individual federal ha sido de cero desde el 1 de enero de 2019.

heading.en: Marketplace Special Enrollment Period (SEP) Triggers for 1099 Contractors
heading.es: Disparadores del Periodo de Inscripcion Especial (SEP) del Mercado para Contratistas 1099

paragraphs.en: ACA Marketplace open enrollment for 2026 coverage runs November 1, 2025 through January 15, 2026 in most states. Outside that window, 1099 independent contractors and freelancers can enroll only through a Marketplace Special Enrollment Period triggered by a qualifying life event. The standard SEP window is 60 days from the date of the qualifying event. Missing that 60-day deadline typically means waiting until the next open enrollment period, which can leave a contract worker uninsured for months. Some events open a window that begins 60 days before the event, allowing contractors who anticipate a coverage loss to pre-enroll.
paragraphs.es: La inscripcion abierta del Mercado ACA para cobertura 2026 se extiende del 1 de noviembre de 2025 al 15 de enero de 2026 en la mayoria de los estados. Fuera de esa ventana, los contratistas independientes 1099 y freelancers solo pueden inscribirse a traves de un Periodo de Inscripcion Especial del Mercado activado por un evento de vida calificado. La ventana estandar del SEP es de 60 dias desde la fecha del evento calificado.

paragraphs.en: How to apply for a Marketplace plan during a Special Enrollment Period: (1) Go to HealthCare.gov or your state's exchange and create or log in to your account. (2) Report the qualifying life event and the date it occurred. (3) Upload or have ready the required verification documents (see list below). (4) Choose a plan and confirm enrollment. (5) Pay your first premium before the deadline shown in your confirmation, or coverage will not activate. Documents typically required for SEP verification include: proof of prior coverage loss (employer letter, COBRA election notice, or loss-of-coverage letter dated within 60 days), proof of the qualifying event (marriage certificate, birth certificate, lease or utility bill for a move), and a government-issued photo ID. Common reasons Marketplace SEP applications are denied: event date outside the 60-day window, supporting documents do not match the event type, the prior coverage was a short-term or non-qualifying plan, or the contractor's reported MAGI falls below 100% FPL in a non-expansion state (the coverage gap).
paragraphs.es: Como solicitar un plan del Mercado durante un Periodo de Inscripcion Especial: (1) Vaya a HealthCare.gov o el intercambio de su estado e inicie sesion en su cuenta. (2) Reporte el evento de vida calificado y la fecha en que ocurrio. (3) Cargue o tenga listos los documentos de verificacion requeridos. (4) Elija un plan y confirme la inscripcion. (5) Pague su primera prima antes de la fecha limite que se muestra en su confirmacion, o la cobertura no se activara. Los documentos que tipicamente se requieren para la verificacion del SEP incluyen: prueba de perdida de cobertura anterior, prueba del evento calificado y una identificacion con foto emitida por el gobierno.

list.en: Loss of employer-sponsored or other qualifying coverage: 60-day SEP window. Leaving a W-2 job to become an independent contractor qualifies.
list.es: Perdida de cobertura patrocinada por el empleador u otra cobertura calificada: ventana SEP de 60 dias. Dejar un trabajo W-2 para convertirse en contratista independiente califica.

list.en: Marriage or entering a domestic partnership: 60-day SEP window from date of marriage or domestic partnership registration.
list.es: Matrimonio o ingreso a una asociacion domestica: ventana SEP de 60 dias desde la fecha del matrimonio o registro de asociacion domestica.

list.en: Birth, adoption, or placement of a foster child: 60-day SEP window. Newborns have a 60-day retroactive enrollment window.
list.es: Nacimiento, adopcion o colocacion de un nino de crianza: ventana SEP de 60 dias. Los recien nacidos tienen una ventana de inscripcion retroactiva de 60 dias.

list.en: Permanent move to a new coverage area or state: 60-day SEP window if the new location has different plan options.
list.es: Mudanza permanente a una nueva area de cobertura o estado: ventana SEP de 60 dias si la nueva ubicacion tiene diferentes opciones de plan.

list.en: Income change that crosses the Medicaid eligibility threshold (up or down): triggers a SEP or a Medicaid enrollment period. Contractors with variable 1099 income may cross this line mid-year.
list.es: Cambio de ingreso que cruza el umbral de elegibilidad de Medicaid (hacia arriba o hacia abajo): activa un SEP o un periodo de inscripcion de Medicaid. Los contratistas con ingresos 1099 variables pueden cruzar este limite a mitad de ano.

list.en: Divorce or legal separation that causes loss of coverage: 60-day SEP window from the date the coverage ends.
list.es: Divorcio o separacion legal que causa perdida de cobertura: ventana SEP de 60 dias desde la fecha en que termina la cobertura.

list.en: Turning 26 and aging off a parent's plan: 60-day SEP window from the birthday. Self-employed and freelance young adults hitting 26 can use this to enter the Marketplace or enroll in Medicaid if income-eligible.
list.es: Cumplir 26 anos y salir del plan de los padres: ventana SEP de 60 dias desde el cumpleanos. Los jovenes adultos autonomos y freelancers que cumplen 26 pueden usar esto para entrar al Mercado o inscribirse en Medicaid si son elegibles por ingresos.

heading.es: Elegibilidad para Planes Catastroficos para Contratistas 1099

paragraphs.en: ACA Marketplace catastrophic plans are available only to individuals under age 30, or to anyone who qualifies for a hardship exemption regardless of age. The 2026 catastrophic plan deductible equals the ACA out-of-pocket maximum for that year: $10,600 for individual coverage. After the deductible is met, preventive care and three primary care visits per year are covered at no cost. Independent contractors and freelancers under 30 who are in good health and primarily want protection against a major medical event may find catastrophic plans attractive, as the premiums are typically the lowest available on the Marketplace. Catastrophic plan premiums do not qualify for the Premium Tax Credit, so contract workers who are income-eligible for subsidies usually come out ahead on a Silver or Bronze plan after applying the credit.
paragraphs.es: Los planes catastroficos del Mercado ACA estan disponibles solo para personas menores de 30 anos, o para cualquier persona que califique para una exencion por dificultades independientemente de la edad. El deducible del plan catastrofico de 2026 es igual al maximo de bolsillo del ACA para ese ano: $10,600 para cobertura individual. Los contratistas independientes y freelancers menores de 30 anos que estan en buena salud y principalmente quieren proteccion contra un evento medico mayor pueden encontrar los planes catastroficos atractivos, ya que las primas son tipicamente las mas bajas disponibles en el Mercado. Las primas de planes catastroficos no califican para el Credito Fiscal de Prima, por lo que los trabajadores por contrato elegibles por ingresos para subsidios generalmente salen adelante con un plan Silver o Bronze despues de aplicar el credito.

paragraphs.en: 1099 contractors over age 30 without a qualifying hardship exemption are not eligible for catastrophic plans. For those contract workers, the lowest-cost ACA option is typically a Bronze HDHP, which combines low premiums with HSA eligibility. The hardship exemption pathways that allow over-30 enrollment in catastrophic plans include documented circumstances such as a recent natural disaster, domestic violence, eviction or foreclosure, or other situations that severely limited the ability to obtain coverage. Applications for hardship exemptions are filed through the Marketplace.
paragraphs.es: Los contratistas 1099 mayores de 30 anos sin una exencion por dificultades calificada no son elegibles para planes catastroficos. Para esos trabajadores por contrato, la opcion ACA de menor costo es tipicamente un HDHP Bronze, que combina primas bajas con elegibilidad para HSA. Las vias de exencion por dificultades que permiten la inscripcion de mayores de 30 en planes catastroficos incluyen circunstancias documentadas como un desastre natural reciente, violencia domestica, desalojo o ejecucion hipotecaria.

en.question: What is the cheapest health insurance option for 1099 contractors in 2026?
en.answer: For independent contractors with MAGI below 400% FPL ($63,840 single in 2026), an ACA Marketplace Silver or Bronze plan with premium tax credits is usually cheapest after the subsidy. For self-employed earners and freelancers above the cliff, a Bronze HSA-qualified HDHP plus a fully funded Health Savings Account is typically the most cost-effective combination. The HDHP has the lowest sticker premium; the HSA contribution ($4,400 self-only in 2026) is deductible above the line; and unused HSA funds roll over year to year. After Form 7206 deducts the premium and the HSA deducts the contribution, effective net cost for a high-earning sole proprietor can fall 30% to 40% from sticker.

en.question: Do 1099 independent contractors qualify for premium tax credits?
en.answer: Yes, if projected 2026 MAGI falls below 400% FPL ($63,840 single, $132,000 family of four). The enhanced ARPA/IRA credits expired January 1, 2026, restoring the subsidy cliff. For freelancers and contract workers with variable income, MAGI is calculated after business expenses, the half-SE-tax deduction, the Form 7206 health insurance deduction, and HSA contributions. A sole proprietor with $85,000 in gross 1099 billings can often land at $60,000 to $65,000 in MAGI after those deductions, which still qualifies for meaningful credits. The IRS reconciles advance credits against actual MAGI on Form 1095-A at tax time.

en.question: Can 1099 contractors deduct health insurance premiums on their taxes?
en.answer: Yes. Form 7206 lets independent contractors and sole proprietors deduct 100% of health insurance premiums above the line on Schedule 1, Line 17. The deduction covers medical, dental, and qualifying long-term care premiums for the contractor, their spouse, and their dependents. Critical caveat: Form 7206 reduces income tax only. It does NOT reduce self-employment tax on Schedule SE. The 15.3% SE tax (12.4% Social Security plus 2.9% Medicare) is calculated on net Schedule C earnings before the health insurance deduction is applied. The deduction also cannot exceed net self-employment income minus the deductible half of SE tax.

en.question: Can 1099 contractors use an HSA?
en.answer: Yes, any 1099 contractor, freelancer, or self-employed worker enrolled in a qualifying HSA-eligible HDHP can open and fund an HSA. For 2026, the contribution limit is $4,400 for self-only coverage or $8,750 for family coverage, plus a $1,000 catch-up if age 55 or older. The HDHP minimum deductible must be $1,700 (self) or $3,400 (family) in 2026 to qualify. HSA contributions are deductible above the line (Form 8889, Schedule 1), reducing MAGI and income tax. Growth is tax-free. Qualified medical withdrawals are tax-free. Flexible Spending Accounts (FSAs) are not available to 1099 contractors because FSAs require an employer sponsor.

en.question: What if a 1099 contractor makes too much to qualify for ACA subsidies?
en.answer: Above 400% FPL ($63,840 single in 2026), independent contractors pay full sticker price for Marketplace plans. The best strategy for high-earning freelancers and sole proprietors is a Bronze HSA-qualified HDHP paired with a maxed Health Savings Account. The HSA contribution ($4,400 self-only / $8,750 family in 2026) is fully deductible. Adding a Solo 401(k) deferral and the Form 7206 deduction on top can stack three above-the-line deductions and potentially bring MAGI back below the subsidy cliff. Contract workers near the 400% FPL line should model their MAGI projection with all four deductions before assuming they're above the cliff.

en.question: When can a 1099 contractor enroll in a Marketplace plan outside open enrollment?
en.answer: A Marketplace Special Enrollment Period (SEP) is triggered by a qualifying life event. For self-employed and independent contractors, the most common SEP triggers are: leaving W-2 employment to go independent (loss of employer coverage, 60-day window), marriage (60 days), birth or adoption of a child (60 days), a permanent move to a new coverage area (60 days), and an income change that crosses the Medicaid eligibility threshold. The SEP window is typically 60 days from the date of the qualifying event. Missing the deadline usually means waiting until the next open enrollment period.

en.question: Does my state offer a healthcare stipend or portable benefits for independent contractors?
en.answer: California's Proposition 22 requires Uber, Lyft, DoorDash, Instacart, and other app-based platforms to pay a quarterly healthcare stipend to drivers and couriers averaging 15+ engaged hours per week. New York's Freelance Isn't Free Act guarantees contract payment rights for freelancers doing work over $800. Massachusetts's Initiative 1 of 2024 extended portable benefit protections for app-based workers. States with individual health insurance mandates (California, Massachusetts, New Jersey, Rhode Island, Vermont, Washington D.C.) require minimum essential coverage or impose a state-level penalty. The federal individual mandate penalty has been zero since 2019.

en.question: Can 1099 contractors under 30 enroll in a catastrophic plan?
en.answer: Yes. ACA Marketplace catastrophic plans are available to anyone under age 30, or to anyone over 30 who qualifies for a documented hardship exemption. The 2026 catastrophic plan deductible is $10,600 individual (equal to the ACA out-of-pocket maximum). Premiums are typically the lowest on the Marketplace, but catastrophic plan premiums do NOT qualify for the premium tax credit. Young freelancers and independent contractors who are income-eligible for subsidies will usually find a Bronze or Silver plan cheaper after applying the credit than a catastrophic plan at full premium.

es.question: Cual es la opcion de seguro medico mas barata para contratistas 1099 en 2026?
es.answer: Para contratistas independientes con MAGI por debajo del 400% FPL ($63,840 individual en 2026), un plan Silver o Bronze del Mercado ACA con creditos fiscales de prima suele ser el mas barato despues del subsidio. Para los autonomos y freelancers por encima del tope, un HDHP Bronze compatible con HSA mas una Cuenta de Ahorros para la Salud totalmente financiada es tipicamente la combinacion mas rentable. El HDHP tiene la prima de etiqueta mas baja; la contribucion al HSA ($4,400 individual en 2026) es deducible above-the-line; y los fondos no utilizados del HSA se acumulan de ano en ano.

es.question: Los contratistas independientes 1099 califican para creditos fiscales de prima?
es.answer: Si, si el MAGI proyectado para 2026 cae por debajo del 400% FPL ($63,840 individual, $132,000 familia de cuatro). Los creditos mejorados de ARPA/IRA expiraron el 1 de enero de 2026, restaurando el tope del subsidio. Para los freelancers y trabajadores por contrato con ingresos variables, el MAGI se calcula despues de los gastos comerciales, la deduccion de la mitad del impuesto SE, la deduccion del Formulario 7206 y las contribuciones al HSA. Un propietario unico con $85,000 en facturacion bruta 1099 a menudo puede llegar a $60,000 a $65,000 en MAGI despues de esas deducciones.

es.question: Los contratistas 1099 pueden deducir las primas de seguro medico en sus impuestos?
es.answer: Si. El Formulario 7206 permite a los contratistas independientes y propietarios unicos deducir el 100% de las primas de seguro medico above-the-line en el Schedule 1, Linea 17. La deduccion cubre las primas medicas, dentales y de cuidado a largo plazo calificadas para el contratista, su conyuge y sus dependientes. Advertencia critica: el Formulario 7206 reduce solo el impuesto sobre la renta. NO reduce el impuesto al trabajo independiente en el Schedule SE. El 15.3% del impuesto SE se calcula sobre las ganancias netas del Schedule C antes de que se aplique la deduccion del seguro medico.

es.question: Los contratistas 1099 pueden usar una HSA?
es.answer: Si, cualquier contratista 1099, freelancer o trabajador autonomo inscrito en un HDHP elegible para HSA puede abrir y financiar una HSA. Para 2026, el limite de contribucion es $4,400 para cobertura individual o $8,750 para cobertura familiar, mas $1,000 adicionales si tiene 55 anos o mas. El deducible minimo del HDHP debe ser $1,700 (individual) o $3,400 (familiar) en 2026 para calificar. Las contribuciones a la HSA son deducibles above-the-line, reduciendo el MAGI y el impuesto sobre la renta. Las Cuentas de Gastos Flexibles (FSA) no estan disponibles para los contratistas 1099 porque las FSA requieren un patrocinador empleador.

es.question: Que pasa si un contratista 1099 gana demasiado para calificar para subsidios del ACA?
es.answer: Por encima del 400% FPL ($63,840 individual en 2026), los contratistas independientes pagan el precio completo por los planes del Mercado. La mejor estrategia para los freelancers y propietarios unicos de altos ingresos es un HDHP Bronze compatible con HSA combinado con una Cuenta de Ahorros para la Salud al maximo. La contribucion al HSA ($4,400 individual / $8,750 familiar en 2026) es totalmente deducible. Agregar un diferimiento del Solo 401(k) y la deduccion del Formulario 7206 puede apilar tres deducciones above-the-line y potencialmente llevar el MAGI de regreso por debajo del tope del subsidio.

es.question: Cuando puede un contratista 1099 inscribirse en un plan del Mercado fuera de la inscripcion abierta?
es.answer: Un Periodo de Inscripcion Especial (SEP) del Mercado es activado por un evento de vida calificado. Para los autonomos y contratistas independientes, los disparadores SEP mas comunes son: dejar el empleo W-2 para ser independiente (perdida de cobertura del empleador, ventana de 60 dias), matrimonio (60 dias), nacimiento o adopcion de un hijo (60 dias), una mudanza permanente a una nueva area de cobertura (60 dias), y un cambio de ingreso que cruza el umbral de elegibilidad de Medicaid. La ventana SEP es tipicamente de 60 dias desde la fecha del evento calificado.

es.question: Mi estado ofrece un estipendio de atencion medica o beneficios portatiles para contratistas independientes?
es.answer: La Proposicion 22 de California requiere que Uber, Lyft, DoorDash, Instacart y otras plataformas basadas en aplicaciones paguen un estipendio trimestral de atencion medica a los conductores y mensajeros que promedian 15 o mas horas de trabajo comprometido por semana. La Ley Freelance No es Gratis de Nueva York garantiza los derechos de pago de contratos para freelancers que realizan trabajo de mas de $800. Los estados con mandatos individuales de seguro medico (California, Massachusetts, Nueva Jersey, Rhode Island, Vermont, Washington D.C.) requieren cobertura esencial minima o imponen una penalidad a nivel estatal. La penalidad del mandato individual federal ha sido cero desde 2019.

es.question: Los contratistas 1099 menores de 30 anos pueden inscribirse en un plan catastrofico?
es.answer: Si. Los planes catastroficos del Mercado ACA estan disponibles para cualquier persona menor de 30 anos, o para cualquier persona mayor de 30 que califique para una exencion documentada por dificultades. El deducible del plan catastrofico de 2026 es $10,600 individual (igual al maximo de bolsillo del ACA). Las primas son tipicamente las mas bajas del Mercado, pero las primas de planes catastroficos NO califican para el credito fiscal de prima. Los jovenes freelancers y contratistas independientes que son elegibles para subsidios por ingresos generalmente encontraran un plan Bronze o Silver mas barato despues de aplicar el credito.
